steps.title: Set up the right triangle and identify the known angle
steps.content: We have a right triangle ABC with the right angle at B. Looking at the figure, the hypotenuse AC makes an angle of 57° at vertex C. We need to find the length of side AB.

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio from angle C
steps.content:
From angle C's perspective in the right triangle, we can use the sine ratio. The side opposite to the 57° angle is AB, and the hypotenuse is AC. Using the definition of sine:

$$\sin(57°) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{AB}{AC}$$

From this equation, we can express AB in terms of the hypotenuse: AB = AC · sin(57°). When we consider the hypotenuse as our reference unit, the length of segment AB is directly given by the expression sin(57°). That matches choice A — A is correct.
